name: TEAM VTO POWERPUSH
description:
⚡️🚴🏼‍♂️💨 POWER PUSH! 🚴🏼‍♂️💨⚡️
Looking for a Group Ride that is different to the rest? You've found it! 👍🏻

We’re building a team...... a TIGHT-KNIT team 💨. We aim to break records over the 7.1km timed Fuego Flats Reverse segment. (The aim of the this ride is to take as many riders as possible with us under 9 mins). We start with a lap and a half to settle the group, recon the course and talk tactics. The hammer drops at about 40 mins and the TEAM TIME TRIAL starts.
As a group we aim to PUSH over 50KPH (31MPH) and hold it all the way to the finish arch. This requires TEAMWORK, POWER and DETERMINATION. You will need to dig deep!

Team VTO value FUN, FRIENDSHIP and DETERMINATION. We welcome athletes from around the world 🙏🏻 🌎

Join our Zwift Club - Team VTO (on your companion app 👉🏻📲 https://www.zwift.com/clubs/151ca95c-f6de-4fe2-ae88-a769e367f439/home

To be involved in our community (+ claim your prizes), join our FB group
www.facebook.com/groups/thenewzwifttrioz/

Absolutely Everyone is welcome! 🙏🏻

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

name: ZMCC EyeballZ
description:
From humble beginnings on Melbourne's Mornington Peninsula, ZMCC has grown to become a favourite hangout within the Zwiftverse. Whether you're looking to push yourself, recover or just spin around - ZMCC is always there for you. Bad dad jokes and questionable banter are just extras you can't buy.

We make the following promises:

We won't yell at the flyers - let them live their imaginary life.
We won't wait for stragglers - we can't help poor event choices or tech issues.
We won't bore or tease you with pretend drinks or snacks.

Join us. Enjoy. You're welcome.

Like and join us on Facebook at https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftmelbournecyclingclub

name: QueenBee Tuesday Tour
description:
Join the QueenBee Ladies social touring ride around Zwiftlands most picturesque scenes. This ride is rubber-banded to keep the everyone together and stress free riding! 

The QueenBees are a group of women who share a love of cycling. Just as a hive works together to bring out the best in every member of the community; so do we. We strive to support, empower and encourage women to live their best lives, no matter where they are on their life, cycling or fitness journey.

name: Restart Esports - Monday Mash
description:
Restart Esports - instagram.com/restart.esports

1. Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) is mandatory for all participants.

2. Power Source is Mandatory for all racers. A smart trainer or smart bike should be your power source where possible. ZPower is not allowed. 

3. Dual Recording - Should always be done if you have the capability. All podium positions are expected to dual record or have at least have a recent dual recording history on their profile. Any rider posting high level performances is expected to validate their hardware with dual recordings as a minimum. 

Results WILL be amended (if required), in the interest of fair play, for all Restart Esports hosted events.

description:

name: L'Apero JZQc
description:
Zwift Email: 

Event Name: L'Apéro JZQc

Existing Event Organizer/Owner: yes,-i-am-an-existing-event-organizer/owner

Name of Team/Club/Organization: JZQc

Number of Weekly Events:

Event description: 🟦L’APÉRO JZQc 🟦
https://jzqc.ca/
🚴‍♂️ Chaque lundi à 17 h 15 – La ride semi-relax de début de semaine !
L’Apéro JZQc est une ride hebdomadaire sociale, semi relax, et toujours ouverte à tous.
C’est l’occasion idéale de se dégourdir avant le souper, de jaser un peu et de commencer la semaine avec une belle ambiance.
🌟 Pourquoi participer ?
Parce que l’Apéro, c’est une ride agréable, motivante et accessible à tout le monde.
On roule pour le plaisir, sans pression, dans un esprit de partage, de fun et d’inclusion.
📍 Parcours & leaders – Une surprise chaque semaine !
Chaque lundi :
• Le parcours change et est généralement semi-vallonné — un juste milieu entre les Mercredis JZQc et les Lundis de grimpe.
• Les leaders changent également pour garder la ride vivante et variée.
⏱️ Détails
• Départ : 17 h 15
• Durée : maximum 70 minutes
• Late Join : Activé
• Ouvert à tous
(Le départ plus tôt permet de rouler AVANT le souper et de garder votre soirée familiale libre.)
⚙️ Rythme & fonctionnement
🔹 Suivre les leaders
Selon la semaine, vous pouvez suivre les leaders :
• en mode autonome, (1,8-2,0wk)
• ou en mode élastique.
Peu importe le mode : on s’amuse et on roule ensemble autant que possible.
🔹 Rythme général
Le rythme de l’Apéro est léger, fluide et accessible, parfait pour décompresser après la journée.
🔹 Liberté totale
Durant l’Apéro, chacun peut :
• suivre le leader,
• rouler plus lentement ou plus vite,
• créer son propre peloton,
• ou rouler à son rythme.
Aucune compétition — juste du plaisir, du partage et de la bonne humeur.
🔹 Pour les motivés !
Les plus audacieux peuvent ensuite enchaîner avec les Lundis de grimpe pour un défi supplémentaire. 🏔️😄
🗣️ Communication
Un canal Discord est disponible pour ceux qui veulent discuter ou échanger pendant la ride.
https://discord.gg/VRZb3YmM

Ce n’est pas une obligation : chacun choisit s’il veut parler ou rester silencieux.
Plusieurs canaux sont offerts pour faciliter les échanges, et tous sont accessibles à ceux qui le souhaitent.
🎯 L’objectif de l’Apéro
Créer chaque semaine une ride :
• relax
• semi-vallonnée
• sociale
• accessible
• motivante
• et ouverte à tous
Avec une ambiance chaleureuse JZQc.
💙🟧 On vous attend pour L’Apéro !
Débutant ou expérimenté, tout le monde est bienvenu.
Chaque lundi à 17 h 15, on se retrouve pour une belle ride de fin d’après-midi.

eventSubgroups.name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit || Advanced (A)

name: The Grind Finale
description:
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

This session aims to replicate the demands of a mountain bike ride / event. 

Mountain biking generally involves far more variations in effort compared with road riding - so plenty of surges and time spent being over geared! We also spend a lot of time on and off the power, so short spells going  hard to get over an obstacle, followed by a freewheel or very low power on a descent or little dip.

We touch upon all of these aspects in todays session, as we start off alternating every 30 seconds between riding hard at a low cadence, and riding easy - as if we are riding through an undulating single track. Then we make a ramp in effort, which finishes with a 30 second near max effort up a steep, rocky banking!

WORKOUT DESIGNER

Matt Rowe - Heavily involved in cycling for the past 25 years, Matt represented Great Britain at the Junior World Championships before graduating to the pro ranks. Matt draws on his extensive race experience to help amateur cyclists around the world achieve their personal goals. 

ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop. 

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level. For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

eventSubgroups.name: The Grind Finale (E)
eventSubgroups.description: #zwiftofficial

FTP Builder 6x3
This one hour session is designed to help improve your FTP. The main set is 6x3 minutes each with its own variation within the 3 minute segments. All will have at least some of the 3-minutes that are above your current FTP. Resetting the bar!

Stress Points are 67.

name: Monday Run Club
description:
Start your week off right with a group run hosted by the Zwift Runners Facebook Group. We’ll ease into another week of training alongside the community while actively recovering from any long runs you might have had during the weekend. With runs in seven time slots with four pace groups each (A being the fastest and D being the slowest), there’s an event for everyone. 

Pace Groups
A) 7.5–8.5 mph // 12–14 kph
B) 7–7.5 mph // 10.5–12 kph
C) 6–6.5 mph // 9.5–10.5 kph
D) 5–5.5 mph // 8–9 kph

We’ll be using workout mode throughout these runs so everyone can easily determine their speed. Though these runs utilize a mostly steady pace, there will be some speed changes for variety.

If you have any questions about this event or want to find out more about Zwift Run in general, then please visit the Zwift Runners Facebook Group or zwift.com/run

name: RO4H Restoration Ride
description:
The RO4H Restoration Ride is a chance to do your body a favor following all the hard riding you did over the weekend. Participating in this low-intensity ride will provide some active recovery to your body and allow your mind to relax in a friendly, casual, supportive, and respectful atmosphere.

This restorative ride is ultimately a social ride, and everyone is welcome to participate. The pace will vary depending on terrain and route but will have an average speed of about 15-18 mph/24-29 kph. We’ll remain sub 2 W/kg on any climbs and climbs will have sub 4% average grade. Despite this being a recovery pace for some, it will be a challenging effort for others. You should be prepared to meet the listed average speed. Being a social ride, we strive to ride together to take advantage of the strong group draft within the peloton. We’ll aim to be a no-drop ride. This ride is certainly not a race, and we discourage fliers.
We are proud of our strong sweeper team. Part of the RO4H ride philosophy is that we do not want anyone to ride alone -- unless they choose so. Our sweepers will either help riders bridge to the peloton or form a second pace group, so you do not ride alone.

So go ahead and join the RO4H Restoration Ride -- where friends are plentiful, and we are Better Together.

Check out the RO4H ride schedule: https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/ro4h.

FB: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZwiftRideOn4Health/.

Discord: https://discord.gg/bWWdqWVkhd

#ro4h

eventSubgroups.name: RO4H Restoration Ride (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
The RO4H Restoration Ride is a chance to do your body a favor following all the hard riding you did over the weekend. Participating in this low-intensity ride will provide some active recovery to your body and allow your mind to relax in a friendly, casual, supportive, and respectful atmosphere.

This restorative ride is ultimately a social ride, and everyone is welcome to participate. The pace will vary depending on terrain and route but will have an average speed of about 15-18 mph/24-29 kph. We’ll remain sub 2 W/kg on any climbs and climbs will have sub 4% average grade. Despite this being a recovery pace for some, it will be a challenging effort for others. You should be prepared to meet the listed average speed. Being a social ride, we strive to ride together to take advantage of the strong group draft within the peloton. We’ll aim to be a no-drop ride. This ride is certainly not a race, and we discourage fliers.
We are proud of our strong sweeper team. Part of the RO4H ride philosophy is that we do not want anyone to ride alone -- unless they choose so. Our sweepers will either help riders bridge to the peloton or form a second pace group, so you do not ride alone.

So go ahead and join the RO4H Restoration Ride -- where friends are plentiful, and we are Better Together.

Check out the RO4H ride schedule: https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/ro4h.

Look us up on FB: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZwiftRideOn4Health/.

#ro4h

name: TTR Team "Prr-suit" Chase Ride
description:
TTR Team "Prr-suit" Chase Ride

Welcome to TTR Team Purr-suit.
Anyone can join.

●This isn’t a social ride. D needs to work to stay away, and C needs to work to catch up.
● Team D will start first, followed by Team C 5mins later.
● Under the guidance of each category leader, the teams will form a tight pack and work together.
● Team D’s goal is to get as far ahead as possible. Team C’s goal is to catch D as quickly as possible
● It’s all about teamwork. Let’s work together and give it our best.

⚠️Caution⚠️
This event runs every other week
On off-weeks, the leader field will be blank and the route will switch to Bologna TT
Please make sure not to confuse the two formats.

name: Ascenders Asia Morning Group Run
description:
Come and join the Ascenders as we put on our running shoes and run
daily with our friends too by starting our day in a healthy way. Research
shows that just 30 minutes of running can have huge benefits on your
short-term and long-term health. So we designed our running events to
motivate athletes to run 30 minutes each day to boost your metabolism and
use those calories. Running will also help you look good and feel stronger.

Our group run is designed with runners of all levels in mind and set the
pace range to be determined by our Zwifter Runners. So find a group pace
that matches how you feel each day and make those steps count for you.
Runners who went beyond the event duration will find that we have selected
courses that will help you earn the Route Badges when you run the full
distance.

So lace up those running shoes as everybody is welcome to join with our
friendly and motivating runners.

# <https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/> Join Ascenders on Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift
# <https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/> Ascenders Discord Channel:
https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: The HERD Mellow Monday
description:
Are you a Herdling with substance? All endurance, not so much about speed? Whether you are trying to get into shape, become a smaller version of yourself or just looking to ride with some like-minded people, this is the ride for you. 
 
The goal to have fun, be comfortable with who we are, and blow off some Monday steam.
Pace is 1.0 – 1.3. Non Clyde/Athena riders are welcome, but recognize this is a place for us to feel welcome and comfortable. We will ride a mix of courses, because just because we are larger, doesn’t mean we can’t climb!

Respect the leader's pace and ride with the yellow beacon. This is NOT A RACE! Stronger riders are encouraged to fall back and help sweep the riders struggling in the back.

Join us on Discord during the ride to participate in audio conversation with one another. It will help the time pass by and give you a space to ask all the questions you have about Zwift and Cycling in general: https://discord.gg/Dr7ZtPV (Please use Push-To-Talk)
Check us out on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/zHerd/

#herd

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Times Square Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Epic Run Crew: I Love Mondays
description:
Happy Monday! Ease into the work week with Epic Run Crew!
This is a 35 minute group run. You are welcome to run at your own pace (AYOP). Chatting is encouraged! Talk about food. Make a funny observation. Whatever. We don’t judge. Just have some fun!
Epic Run Crew is based in Vancouver, BC, Canada. We were founded as an in-person club in 2021 and then joined Zwift in June 2022.
Use the Zwift Companion app to join the Epic Run Crew club, for access to exclusive member-only group runs.
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/epicruncrew2/
Instagram: @epicruncrew

description:

During ZRL season we will be riding the course that corresponds with that week’s race. The pace will be between 1.5wkg-1.9wkg throughout the duration of the route.

ABOUT THIS SERIES OF EVENTS

From riders new to cycling, to those who have experience racing we want to make your experience fun! If you’re interested in checking out our race team please see our Facebook page Draftingdinos race team on Zwift.

description:

Ideal for newer riders, recovery days, or anyone wanting a calm and consistent effort.

Mondays | ~60 mins | (1.2–1.6 w/kg)

🌍
Open to all | Steady flat pace

💬
Friendly group riding principles

🎁
Ride to unlock the ZSUN kit

Start your week right – together, steady, and strong.

This is a group ride that targets people who want a supportive rides that keeps to a steady pace (1.5w/kg), which will not exceed 18-19mph (29-30kph) on the flat parts of the course.

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Neon After Party
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER?

name: Cheesecake Maple Shots
description:
Cheesecake Maple Shots is a fun group ride at an average power of around 3W/kg, but with some optional sprints along the way! Fueled by the very best maple syrup that Canada has to offer (plus a selection of other North American treats), it is a great way to build up your sprint power! 

For those who just want to ride in the draft with the yellow beacon, then this is totally fine also. There is usually plenty of chat in the bunch for those who enjoy the social side or riding. And if you've got anything left in your legs at the end you should feel fee to join in an optional "embrace the burn" gradual lifting of the pace for the final minutes! 

An awesome way to have fun and improve fitness at the same time! Late join available for up to 30 mins after the ride start. All welcome!

name: ZTBR - Sprint Training [CRISterium]
description:
Come enjoy ZTBR’s weekly sprint session!
We roll at 2W/kg and It is ‘all out’ when we hit a sprint segment!
Different routes each week.

Chase your PRs and challenge others for the green jersey!

ZTBR’s beacons promote regrouping/recovery rolling  at maximum 2w/kg after every segment.
We instruct all riders to stay together for best segment times and  fun.
 
Join us on Instagram @zwiftteambrazil

---

PORTUGUÊS:
Venha aproveitar a sessão semanal de sprint do ZTBR!
Rolamos a 2W/kg e está “total” quando atingimos um segmento de sprint!
Rotas diferentes a cada semana.

Persiga seus PRs e desafie outros pela camisa verde!

Os beacons da ZTBR promovem reagrupamento/recuperação rolando no máximo 2w/kg após cada segmento.
Instruímos todos os pilotos a permanecerem juntos para obter os melhores momentos e diversão do segmento.
 
Junte-se a nós no Instagram @zwiftteambrazil

Good training!

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (OverTheEdge OTE)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is hosted by OverTheEdge (OTE) (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/8b2c3be7-a30d-403f-a9b9-0de8353518c4).

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You’ll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that’s rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you’ll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL’s website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wtrl #zrl #ote

name: Monday Run Club
description:
Start your week off right with a group run hosted by the Zwift Runners Facebook Group. We’ll ease into another week of training alongside the community while actively recovering from any long runs you might have had during the weekend. With runs in seven time slots with four pace groups each (A being the fastest and D being the slowest), there’s an event for everyone. 

Pace Groups
A) 7.5–8.5 mph // 12–14 kph
B) 7–7.5 mph // 10.5–12 kph
C) 6–6.5 mph // 9.5–10.5 kph
D) 5–5.5 mph // 8–9 kph

We’ll be using workout mode throughout these runs so everyone can easily determine their speed. Though these runs utilize a mostly steady pace, there will be some speed changes for variety.

If you have any questions about this event or want to find out more about Zwift Run in general, then please visit the Zwift Runners Facebook Group or zwift.com/run

#gozwiftrun #mrc

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: VO2 Max 40/20
description:
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

Today, we'll be aiming for around 16mins of VO2 Max time and incorporating a 40/20 interval approach which will challenge you for sure! Let's make it happen today! REMINDER: You can always adjust the FTP bias via the arrow keys on your keyboard, or the Zwift Companion app. Once you get over threshold, using a %FTP for workout prescription can get murky, so always adjust as needed

WORKOUT DESIGNER

Shayne Gaffney (GC Coaching) holds a bachelors degree in Health Science in Professional Development and Advanced Patient Care, is a USA Cycling Level 1 (expert level) Certified Coach, a level 2 certified Training Peaks coach, a USA Cycling certified power based training coach, USA Olympic Committee Safe Sport Certified, and a licensed physical therapist assistant.  He is also the creator of Zwift's "Build Me Up" Flexible Training Plan.  He can be contacted directly via  for any cycling or training related questions.

For more information on GC Coaching, please visit www.gaffneycyclingcoaching.com

ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop. 

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level. For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

name: Project Echelon Veterans Honor Ride
description:
"Join Project Echelon Racing in a social recover ride to connect and support active service members and veterans with their comrades and community advocates.

Project Echelon Racing is a UCI Continental team with a mission to educate, equip, and empower veterans through physical activity. The team believes that cycling is a form of mental and physical therapy that has the power to connect people in a way that can trasform lives. The organization works to support over 500 military veterans each year, providing coaching, mentoring, and community building services, as well as financial supports to help remove accessibility barriers to the sport.

Learn more at www.ProjectEchelonRacing.com

name: Good Time Riders - Pursuit Race
description:
Good Time Riders are a group of Zwifters from across the world who enjoy
riding, training and racing together. We race hard, encourage one another
to be their best, but enjoyment comes first. We have active teams at all
levels racing in Club Ladder, TTT, ZRL and DIRT series. We also run a
regular sub2 group ride for the Zwift community on Mondays, and a club
workout on Fridays. New members are always welcome, you can find us on the
Companion App, Facebook and Discord.

Enjoy the race!

name: ZLDR Easy 30
description:
Join Zwift Long-Distance Runners (ZLDR) run club for a relaxed 30 minute run. Whether you want to use this time to walk just to get your blood flowing or push for a new personal best or anything in between, this is a great session to join! Even if you can’t join for 30 minutes, come for whatever you have available. We are a flexible group of walkers/runners who want to help you get consistent and get results.

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/

https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn

description:
Want to improve your 5K run time. Join us regularly for this ladder workout and watch your results improve.

We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.” – Aristotle<https://everydaypower.com/aristotle-quotes/>

Join for the challenge and group run fun!

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (OTR)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is led by  OTR (On The Rivet) (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/45a345d5-58cd-4e6b-b4f6-4f10531967a9).
Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You'll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that's rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you'll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL's website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wtrl #zrl #otr

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Neon After Party
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Times Square Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Cycle Nation Nightcrawler Ride
description:
Are you a Zwifter looking for a steady, enjoyable late-night or early morning group ride to boost your endurance and aerobic fitness? Look no further than the Cycle Nation Nightcrawler Ride, designed specifically for riders who prefer a consistent pace at sub 1.8w/kg. Scheduled twice a week, this ride is perfect for those aiming to build their stamina and improve their overall fitness.
Our Nightcrawler Ride offers a relaxed, even-paced group ride environment where you can focus on being consistent about your training without the pressure of a race breaking out. A dedicated ride leader will be present to guide the pace, ensuring everyone stays together as a group. This ride is ideal as a warm-up, leading seamlessly into the slightly faster-paced Cycle Nation Joburg & Berlin Sunrise rides on Tuesday and Thursday mornings.

With a 10-minute gap between the rides, you'll have just enough time to refill your bottles and get ready for the next challenge. Join us for the Cycle Nation Nightcrawler Ride and make the most of your early morning or late-night rides! Perfect for anyone looking for a weekly early morning or late-night group.

Connect with Cycle Nation:

• Visit our website: www.cyclenation.co.za
• Join our Facebook Group: https://bit.ly/38TTtAh
• Join our Zwift Team: https://bit.ly/3D2Q4zL
• Join our Strava Club: https://bit.ly/3pEDkFM
• Follow us on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3n4uhft
• Follow us on Instagram: https://bit.ly/3pEsdN6

name: Aktivitus 1 h Group workout
description:
Lets get stronger together! Welcome to join team eSRT for a group workout. 
Each workout is 60-70 minutes and includes fun & hard intervalls designed to improve VO2max, power and endurance. Different workout each week- we have picked the best work outs from of Zwift academy and Aktivitus. A party you definetly want to join!

name: ZLDR AYOP Event
description:
Start your day on the right foot or the left. Come to our 5k at your own pace (AYOP). Run, walk, chat, laugh and get your day moving on a positive note! Attitude is 90% of any situation so let’s make this day the best day and get you in the best mindset so you can be the best you!

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn
ZLDR website for Challenge Months: https://www.zldr.club
Online shop: https://www.sweatydino.com

name: Vitality for UNICEF Group Ride
description:
Vitality, in partnership with UNICEF, is teaming up with Zwift for a series of great social rides for a fantastic cause. Don’t miss out. Join our social rides of 45 minutes to one hour and do your part to give kids a head start. Keeping you active and helping Vitality and UNICEF vaccinate millions of children worldwide.

We encourage everyone to stay together for this social ride. Every ride completion will help UNICEF vaccinate a child against polio.

About Vitality:
 
Guided by a core purpose of making people healthier, Vitality is the leader in shared-value by creating healthy behavior changes in insurance. By blending technology, data, incentives, and behavioural science, Vitality inspires healthy changes in individuals and organizations. 

In partnership with UNICEF, Vitality aims to vaccinate millions of children against polio by donating vaccines on behalf of participants who complete the Vitality for UNICEF Ride series events.

Furthermore, the partnership with Zwift has been established as part of Vitality’s global pledge to get 100 million people 20% more active by 2025 through incentives to live healthier, more active lifestyles.

name: Team Vitality Group Social Ride
description: This is a medium paced, fun ride at the bottom to middle of the C category. It is not a race, but there will be times we can have a go at the Sprints or KOM’s for bit of fun. The average speed for the ride is aprx 32kph and the average watts/kilo is 2.0 to 2.4 each week, although the nature of the courses can cause this to vary. We aim for a steady ride with moments for recovery after crunch moments.

name: The Zombie 5k
description:
Brought to you from the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) this event is called the Zombie Run as for those in the UK this event is in the middle of the night.

Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join, you can run at whatever pace you like, it's not a race.
If you don't want to run 5k then come along for as long as you wish, the support is always appreciated.
The event runs at the same time each day, Tuesday to Saturday and each day there is a different course.

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/

https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Follow Stuart on Strava - https://www.strava.com/athletes/2480515

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: INC Relentless Intervals
description:
This is a Team Time Trial Training session. Expect a hard interval session. The front of this ride will suit Category A riders, however other categories are welcome and encouraged to join and complete the workout in your own gruppettos.

These are no sweep training rides; if you drop from the group, simply continue the workout on your own or with others of a similar pace.
This is an Innovation Cycling Training Ride Event – all riders are welcome. These events are put together to push you further than before, if you are feeling fresh hop on the front, if you need rest you can draft!

name: Cycle Nation Joburg Sunrise
description:
Welcome to the Cycle Nation Joburg Sunrise ride. Join us for an easy morning coffee ride aimed at Zwifters looking for an easy paced morning group ride. All riders are welcome to join the event and enjoy the friendly atmosphere. Please follow the ride leaders instructions and try group around the beacon. Most importantly this isn't a race and we ask that you respect the pace set by the ride leader which will allow for an enjoyable group ride experience for all.

Join our Facebook Group: https://bit.ly/38TTtAh
Join our Zwift Team: https://bit.ly/3D2Q4zL
Join our Strava Club: https://bit.ly/3pEDkFM
Twitter: https://bit.ly/3n4uhft
Instagram: https://bit.ly/3pEsdN6

name: Team Italy - Smile! It's Tuesday Ride
description:
Join the Team Italy Smile! It's Tuesday Ride each week, we’ll roll as a group at a moderate 2.5-3 w/kg pace. The course varies each week, with a range of undulating routes. These rides are perfect towards Zwifters that are looking for a varied content one hour session that can give them a full 5 zone workout. Draft in the group and stay zone 1-3, or move to the front and join the sprints for zones 4 and 5 for short bursts.

Our sweeper team will help you, if requested. Experience with group rides is of advantage - we will use the power of the group to go fast, please follow the Leader (yellow) istructions, the fence is the red barrier behind the leader, if you overtake for 60 seconds it will kick out of the ride, so respect the Leader pace, if you have extra watts you can help our sweepers Team. 

Please Add the tag Ita into your surname. When you finished the ride You will earn the Team Italy Jersey, check your Zwift Garage. If you like our events join our Companion Club Team Italy,  Facebook Group https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftitaly  Telegram https://t.me/ZwiftTeamItaly
#TeamItaly #Respect4Leader

name: Vikings Valhalla Tuesday Climb
description:
Welcome to the ‘Vikings Valhalla Tuesday Climb’. Are you ready to do some climbing at a pace that suits you and have a great time as well? We will supply quality leads and sweeps, all you need to do is be ready to sweat! We have two groups to accommodate different rider levels, if you find yourself in "no mans land" jump in and help sweep to get that little extra burn. Our rides are very inclusive with a "no one gets dropped" approach. We encourage riders to respect the leader's pace and ride with the yellow beacon. This is NOT A RACE! As mentioned, stronger riders are encouraged to fall back and help sweep the riders struggling in/off the back. If you are looking for a fun ride with a great group of people then, this is the
perfect ride for you!

For more information about our rides go to: https://www.facebook.com/groups/503388660165279/

name: Hercules - Breakfast Club
description:
Start your day right with the Breakfast Club — a super friendly and inclusive group ride on Zwift! We ride with the rubber band enabled, so no one gets dropped, and everyone stays together no matter the pace.

Expect great chat, good vibes, quizzes, fun facts and a welcoming atmosphere for all riders, whether you're new to Zwift or a seasoned pro.

Please join our Zwift Club at https://www.zwift.com/uk/clubs/d6581e47-deb2-46ed-a278-5777d5d2fd0f/home

Please join our Facebook Group at https://www.facebook.com/groups/2606407819659263/

If you would like to own our famous Pink & Black Club Kit in real life, please order at https://tinyurl.com/herculestrirun

name: EVO CC Vortex Interval Ride [~3-3.8w/kg avg]
description:
Welcome to the Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] interval session. These rides are geared towards Zwifters that are looking for a varied content, one hour session that can give them a full 5 zone workout. Draft in the group and stay zone 1-3, or move to the front and join the sprints for zones 4 and 5 for short bursts. Average for this ride will be mid to high Category B but it may vary based upon course/leader. There will be keepers at the back, and they will try hard to save dropped riders. The group is based upon high social interaction so bring your best banter. Use the mobile app or a keyboard to communicate in-ride.

#evo

name: EVO CC Flux Ride [1.8 - 2.0w/kg avg]
description:
This is a Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] Social Event and ride for all. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that all riders respect the pace set by the leader (Yellow Beacon). We may include intervals over and below the ride average advertised power range, but we will allow enough recovery and time to regroup after every effort. This is not a race, so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong we would welcome any help offered by stronger riders to bring any dropped riders back to the yellow beacon. We welcome good chat and a social atmosphere for new and experienced Zwifters alike, everyone is welcome.

#evo

name: Team Valhalla Early/Late Everybody's In Spin
description:
Welcome everyone short, tall, large, small and in between to our Tuesday Team Valhalla Early/Late Everybody’s In Spin.

This is D category GROUP ride with the aim to ride as a group at a pace that is inclusive and empathetic to everyone from the seasoned rider to the first timer. The advertised pace is 1.6-2W/KG.

Do you have tired legs or are you early morning pre-espresso fatigued - then this is the ride for you.

Is it a late night or afternoon and you are getting ready for supper or bed - then this is the ride for you.

Are you new to Zwift or Group Rides and worry about getting dropped or worry about a Lead that doesn’t care to help those out back? - then this is the ride for you.

This is a “no-drop” “GROUP” ride around D or Recovery pace so if you do find yourself off the back then just shout out early and there will always be a friendly face to keep you company and help get you back to the Yellow Beacon. All you have to bring is your smile. If you are a stronger rider looking for an extra challenge we would encourage you to help sweep riders off the back.

The 6 goals of this group ride are simple: 1) ride near/with the yellow beacon, 2) enjoy each other’s company/make new friends, 3) be positive, 4) engage in the banter, 5) Big the Blob and 6) HAVE FUN.

We want this ride and all our Vikings and Valhalla Rides to be a great ride experience for Everyone!!

Please check out our Facebook group at: https://www.facebook.com/groups/503388660165279/?ref=share

eventSubgroups.name: Team Valhalla Early/Late Everybody's In Spin (D)

name: SZR Early Birdies
description:
Welcome to SZR Early birdies

This is a group ride with the focus on remaining together. You should aim to sit between the yellow and red beacon. Follow ride leader instructions at all times.

We will ride at an average pace of 2.5 w/kg prior to any race finish. The race finish is at the discretion of the ride leader and will vary in length dependent on the ride.

If there are any sprints allowed during the ride please regroup on the yellow beacon immediately after.

After hills we will focus on getting the back of the group together with the front.

#SZR

name: 3R Octane Interval Ride [~3.2-3.6 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. 3R Octane runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 3.2-3.8 W/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides, including weekly recovery, or transitional, rides which are usually on a Monday. Short, high intensity intervals, longer sweetspot intervals, flying laps, long and short climbs and exploring little used routes all feature.

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some can be up to two hours long, and all end with a mini race.

The Group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the Octane discord server and features a super bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

#Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise #3r

name: 3R SPARK Steady State Ride [~2.0-2.2 w/kg avg]
description: This is a 3R Group Ride Event (not a race). 3R Spark runs five days a week and is designed to be an accessible and sociable training session, aimed primarily at riders in Cat-D. Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.0-2.5 W/kg. The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides. Mondays and Tuesdays are our entry-level steady-state rides, where we aim to hold a steady 2.0-2.2 throughout. Thursdays and Fridays are our interval sessions, designed to help Cat D riders push themselves a little further. For interval sessions we aim to average between 2.2-2.5 for the session, but there will be times above and below. Thursday and Friday sessions often include an optional mini-race at the end. Wednesday is the Taylor Zwift Fan Club ride, a led by the hosts of the popular podcast of the same name – expect a sociable steady-state session with lots to keep you entertained! The TZFC ride is livestreamed at twitch.tv/TaylorZwiftFC. Rides are typically around an hour long. Spark is a great introduction to group rides on Zwift, we are a friendly and supportive group who love to see one another succeed. We always have a sweeper (red beacon) whose wheel you can grab if you get dropped. There’s always plenty of good chat, via in-ride messages or on Discord. Come and join us! Join the 3R Facebook group for more info: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team 3R #3R #RideRaceRaise

name: 3R VOLT Interval Ride [~2.6-3.2 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. 3R VOLT runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.8-3.2 w/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides and training needs, the following is a typical week:
Monday – After what has hopefully been a fun backed weekend on the bike, this our recovery session
Tuesday – Tends to be more of a classic interval session, riders usually finish with an average of around 3.0w/kg
Wednesday – We take to the hills and one of our tougher sessions with riders averaging around 3.2w/kg
Thursday – is our no red beacon ride and again tough interval sets and riders finishing around 3.2w/kg
Friday - is a little different, we don't take ourselves too seriously and often play a variety of games and quizzes along side the intervals and riders end up with around 2.8-3.0w/kg

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some may take up to 75mins with the majority finishing with either a ramp or a mini race.

The group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the VOLT discord server and features a friendly bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

This group ride is not a race so. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: GXY SURGE [1.6-2.5wkg] – CAT D
description:
Welcome to GXY Surge!
 
Different ride leaders will target varying effort levels each day, yet still bring you home well within the advertised power!
 
The ride is looking to push Cat Ds and low Cat Cs that little further all within a manageable 50 minute ride.  Please respect the leader’s pace and watch your fitness develop!
 
GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed.  We are looking forward to riding with you.
 
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally via our Facebook link or by the email below:
 
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

eventSubgroups.name: GXY SURGE [1.6-2.5wkg] – CAT D (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
This is a GXY CC group ride (not a race). GXY Solar Surge is designed to be a sociable training session so come
warmed up ready to work! 

Different ride leaders will target varying effort levels taking riders through ramps, pyramids, sprints and sweet spot training with an occasional recovery ride thrown in forbalance.

The ride is aimed at pushing CAT D and low CAT C riders that little bit further all within a 50 min workout.
We recover EASY and we work HARD! Respect the leader’s pace which will change subject to the
workload, follow the session, work as a team together and watch your fitness develop.

GXY CC are a friendly and supportive team who strive to help others succeed.

Discover more about GXY CC by visiting https://www.Facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: PAOLO BARBERA FEARLESS
description:
Please visit www.heycoach.it or contact me 

Coach Paolo Barbera is a Certified Triathlon and Ironman Coach and the founder of Active Kids, a sporting clinic to help children and adults in the prevention and treatment of obesity through healthy nutrition and tailored training programmes.

Paolo is an experienced triathlete and cyclist. In 2017 he opened an innovative endurance training centre in milan, Tri60, specialized in indoor cycling.

It has been proven that Indoor cycling plays a key role to build strength, resistance and improve overall fitness and technical skills.

Paolo’s workouts are designed to mix different training zones aiming to maximize results in a very short time. Athletes following his workouts, at the right intensity and at the right cadence, have achieved surprising results.

Paolo’s indoor cycling workouts have different lengths to meet every athlete needs.

In the last ten years Paolo has developed very successful training programs for triathletes and cyclists

IT IS NOT HOW YOU START BUT HOW YOU FINISH!

GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normaL event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests to face every workout at the right difficulty level.

name: Compass Community Cycling Thriving Tuesday
description:
Join our social group ride on Zwift — steady effort, good company, and just enough challenge to keep it interesting.

We’ll ride together through a mix of intervals: some short and sharp, others longer and steadier. It’s about turning the pedals, keeping each other going, and sharing the miles.

Each session usually runs two weeks in a row so you can ride it once, then ride it again and notice the difference. Little by little, you’ll find the effort feels smoother, the breathing steadier, the legs that bit stronger.

The chat is open, the music is loud, and there’s always room for some laughs along the way. Drop into the conversation, share how it’s going, or just sit in and spin — it’s your ride.

Recovery isn’t just for the legs. It’s a mental reset, too.





⚠️ Important note
• This is a social group ride, not a formal coaching session.
• Everyone rides at their own pace and you join at your own risk. Please ride within your personal limits and listen to your body.
• The leader and club are volunteers and accept no responsibility for injury, illness, or equipment issues arising from participation.

Show up. Spin steady. Grow stronger together. See you on the start line.

name: INC Relentless Intervals
description:
Suitable for A riders as a base session, or B & C riders wanting to push themselves.

Examples of the drills are – Z2 with 15sec Z5/6 bursts every 6minutes, alternating Z2/Z3 in various durations, Z2 - Z4 ramps of decreasing duration and many other sessions.  

All sessions will be around 60-90mins and will include a warm up so just grab your bottles and head straight to the pen.

This is an Innovation Cycling Training Ride Event – all riders are welcome. These events are put together to push you further than before, if you are feeling fresh hop on the front, if you need rest surf the draft. This training ride is part of the INC RELENTLESS training rides that happen daily with each day focusing on a different area of improvement. 

Tips: Use the draft to rest and ensure you do not lose the draft.

name: NGNM UNITED WE ARE Women's Workout Series
description:
Join our United We Are 5-Month Women’s Training Plan — a progressive program to get you ready for any real-life cycling challenge, from NGNM's United We Are Tour to gravel events, granfondos, or your next big goal.

You’ll build endurance, strength, and climbing power through 14 weeks of structured base work + 5 weeks of focused climbing training.

Train with **Mim Taylor - 2025 UK National Gravel Champion & UCI Worlds multi-podium athlete -** and Milly De Mori, NGNM’s founder.
You show up. We guide the way.
Because training solo is tough — but with United We Are, everything gets easier. 💪

⚡️🚴‍♀️ Event Details:

Workouts: < 1 hour

Community: We stay together—whatever your watts or FTP

Chat: Join us on Discord https://discord.gg/wywQsUaRYX
 or Zwift Companion's chat for support & motivation

💥 BONUS: Unlock NGNM’s exclusive WRL Kit on Zwift!

👉 Join NGNM’s United We Are Cycling Club on Companion:
https://www.zwift.com/eu/clubs/19908def-e39c-466b-9737-4db8bb40e503/join

—————
About No Gods No Masters® (NGNM) — Smart Cycling Apparel for Women
NGNM is more than a cycling brand: it’s a movement empowering women through style, performance, and freedom on&off the bike. Alongside high-end apparel, NGNM runs United We Are—exclusive road and gravel events across Italy and Europe, designed to bring women riders together on unforgettable adventures.
https://nogodsnomasters.life/pages/united-we-are-events

Order the RL of the NGNM jersey on Zwift here:
https://nogodsnomasters.life/collections/ngnm-x-zwift

description: This is a weekday ride that rides for 90 mins and for no less than 20 miles. So grab your bottles and trainer, let's ride! All riders are welcome to ride with us. This ride is put together to push you further than before and build your endurance. if you are feeling strong, push to the front and drag the group. If not, just sit in the peloton.

name: Bens Bash
description:
Training ride open to all. The aim of these rides is to provide a regular training ride for all to join in, riding at a steady pace that for some will feel challenging but over time you will get fitter and more comfortable.

Be mindful of your pace and try to keep the group together rather than just ride off on your own - look at this as a way to improve your fitness as well as pick up some skills around riding with others. 

Try not to ride over 3.5 w/kg but if you are feeling frisky - feel free to open the taps with 10 mins to go. 

We will vary the routes so check back regularly.

Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: SZR Sunrise Ride
description:
Welcome to SZR Sunrise

C and D Ride
Tempo ride with Intervals
Sprints and intervals will be added at the discretion of the leader.
We will at times ride above and below the advertised pace, please don’t feel the need to tell the leader they will be aware but the ride average will be as advertised.
This is NOT a recovery ride, we work hard and work as a team.

E Ride
The focus is on the Group and keeping together while having fun to make the time fly fast!
The ride tempo is shown next to the ride category, this is rider weight / gender dependant.
Note that the average is for the ride prior to any optional faster finish (with a beacon).
The ride leader will aim (prior to any faster finish) to ride mid parameters after a short warmup period.
There might be slight variations above or below the average during the ride.


NB:
Please always respect the ride leader and follow his / her instructions.
The ride tempo is shown next to the ride category, this is rider weight / gender dependant.
